channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: So long, Park City. Sundance Film Festival to relocate to Boulder, Colorado, in 2027
item.description: After a yearlong search, the Sundance Film Festival announced Thursday that its new home will be Boulder, Colorado, keeping Sundance in the mountains but moving it out of Park City, the Utah ski town that had for decades provided the premier independent film gathering its picturesque snowy backdrop.
item.dc:creator: JAKE COYLE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Massachusetts mayor who shuttered a libelous newspaper faces renewed corruption allegations
item.description: A few months ago, a mayor in Massachusetts was relishing a rare victory over a newspaper for its years of personal attacks.
item.dc:creator: Michael Casey, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Turkish student at Tufts University is latest Palestinian supporter swept up in US crackdown
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — A Turkish student detained by federal officers as she walked along a street in a Boston suburb is the latest supporter of Palestinian causes to be swept up in the Trump administration’s crackdown on immigrants who express their political views.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, JAKE OFFENHARTZ and KATHY McCORMACK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Another federal judge blocks Trump policy banning transgender troops in the military
item.description: TACOMA, Wash. (AP) — A U.S. judge in Washington state has blocked enforcement of President Donald Trump’s order banning transgender people from serving in the military, the second nationwide injunction against the policy in as many weeks. The order Thursday from U.S.
item.dc:creator: GENE JOHNSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: At a glance: Who has been detained or deported in US crackdown on pro-Palestinian protesters
item.description: Supporters of Palestinian causes with ties to American universities have been detained in the Trump administration’s crackdown on immigrants who have expressed their political views.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Organ transplant patient in Michigan dies from rabies
item.description: A Michigan resident died earlier this year after contracting rabies from an organ transplant, health officials said.
item.dc:creator: DEVNA BOSE AP Health Writer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Judge says heat in Texas prisons unconstitutional as states face mounting lawsuits
item.description: AUSTIN, Texas (AP) — A federal judge's rebuke of Texas housing prisoners in lockups without air conditioning stopped short of ordering a fix before summer in what has become of the country's biggest lawsuits over keeping prisoners safe during dangerous extreme heat. But U.S.
item.dc:creator: JIM VERTUNO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Here are the international students and faculty known to be targeted by ICE
item.description: The Trump administration’s crackdown on foreign students and faculty who have voiced support for the Palestinian cause has escalated, with universities across the country seeing students either arrested by Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) or having their visas revoked.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5

item.title: NY county clerk refuses to file Texas' fine for doctor accused of prescribing abortion pills
item.description: A county clerk in New York refused Thursday to file a more than $100,000 judgment from Texas against a doctor accused of prescribing abortion pills to a woman near Dallas, setting up a potential challenge to laws designed to shield abortion providers who serve patients in states with abortion bans.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL HILL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Popular corn chips recalled over undeclared allergen
item.description: The popular chip brand Frito-Lay is recalling a limited number of Tostitos tortilla chips over an undeclared allergen.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Langenfeld, KTLA 5

item.title: Prosecutors in the killings of 4 Idaho students seek to use man's crime scene paper as evidence
item.description: Prosecutors in the case of a man charged in the killings of four University of Idaho students are seeking to use as evidence a college paper he wrote that shows his knowledge of crime scenes, according to a court filing this week Bryan Kohberger, 30, is accused in the stabbing deaths of Ethan Chapin
item.dc:creator: ADRIAN SAINZ, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Toddler sneaks onto White House grounds
item.description: A toddler slipped through the White House fence on Wednesday, the Secret Service said in a post on the social platform X. “Just after 6:30 p.m. on Wednesday, U.S. Secret Service Uniformed Division officers observed a child slip through the White House north fence.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, KTLA 5

item.title: Judge: High heat in Texas prisons unconstitutional
item.description: A federal judge found the extreme heat in Texas prisons is “plainly unconstitutional” but did not order the state to begin installing air conditioners.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KRON4

item.title: Bestselling author of 'The Vampire Diaries' dies at 66
item.description: Bestselling author L.J. Smith, best known for the book series "The Vampire Diaries," which also became a TV hit, has died at 66.
item.dc:creator: Laurel Stone, KRON4

item.title: Trump administration revokes state and local health funding
item.description: The Department of Health and Human Services is canceling tens of billions of dollars in federal grants that state and local health departments were using to track infectious diseases, health disparities, vaccinations, mental health services, and other health issues.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump shifts Greenland approach amid blowback, Signal scandal
item.description: Planned trips this week to Greenland’s capital, Nuuk, and a dogsledding race were canceled in favor of a more limited trip to visit American military installations on the island.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Million-dollar lottery ticket was nearly thrown out, winner says
item.description: “I was actually kind of going to throw them away, because I said ‘I’ve never won the lottery,’” said Joaquin Vaughn Redo.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, WFLA, NBC

item.title: New Social Security clawback policy starts this week: What to know
item.description: A major change in how the Social Security Administration (SSA) treats its overpayments is set to kick in Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Chance of Laken Riley's illegal immigrant killer doing time in El Salvador prison addressed by lawmakers
item.description: Could Jose Ibarra, Laken Riley's convicted killer, be deported to an El Salvador prison where the Trump administration just sent hundreds of Venezuelan gang members?
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Army wife pleads guilty to fatally stabbing infant son in the neck at Georgia military base
item.description: A military wife has pleaded guilty to second-degree murder in the 2023 murder of her infant son at Fort Eisenhower in Georgia, prosecutors said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Turkish student at Tufts University detained, video shows masked people handcuffing her
item.description: SOMERVILLE, Mass. (AP) — A Turkish national and doctoral student at Tufts University has been detained by U.S. Department of Homeland Security agents without explanation, her lawyer said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ, KATHY McCORMACK and MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Washington researchers warn of serious decline in honey bee colonies in 2025
item.description: Losses of commercial honey bee colonies are projected to be the highest on record in the last decade, according to Washington State University researchers.
item.dc:creator: Jenna Deml, KRON4

item.title: Family unknowingly had rare, valuable painting for 150 years
item.description: A painting hanging in a French family's living room turned out to be far more valuable than just another trinket or heirloom.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, Fox 5

item.title: Kansas measles cases double to 23 and new Ohio outbreak sickens 10
item.description: A measles outbreak in Kansas doubled in less than a week to 23 cases and has "a possible link” to outbreaks in Texas and New Mexico that have sickened more than 370, the state health department said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: DEVI SHASTRI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Chewing gum releases thousands of microplastics into body, study finds
item.description: A recent study found that chewing gum can release up to thousands of microplastics per piece and potentially be ingested, according to researchers.
item.dc:creator: Rhea Caoile, KRON4

item.title: Trump places 25% tariff on imported autos, expecting to raise $100 billion in tax revenues
item.description: White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt said the tariffs would be detailed at a 4 p.m. EST news conference.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Major department store to shutter some locations in California, other states
item.description: Kohl's announced 27 underperforming stores would close before April. The final day for those locations is coming this weekend.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KTLA 5

item.title: Hawaii doctor accused of trying to kill his wife during hike
item.description: Police say the man tried to push his wife off the trail and allegedly hit her in the head multiple times with a rock.
item.dc:creator: Jill Kuramoto, KRON4

item.title: Supreme Court upholds Biden rule requiring serial numbers and background checks for ghost guns
item.description: The Supreme Court on Wednesday upheld a Biden administration regulation on the nearly impossible-to-trace weapons called ghost guns, clearing the way for continued serial numbers, background checks and age verification requirements to buy them in kits online.
item.dc:creator: Lindsay Whitehurst, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Supreme Court upholds Biden’s ‘ghost guns’ crackdown
item.description: The Supreme Court upheld the Biden administration’s regulation of “ghost guns” in a 7-2 decision Wednesday, rejecting a challenge brought by gun rights groups and several manufacturers.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, ABC 4

item.title: NPR, PBS facing DOGE subcommittee over alleged liberal lean
item.description: At stake are potential cost cuts for both companies, which are partially funded by the federal government.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, Fox 5

item.title: Pilot and 2 young daughters survive the night on airplane wing after crashing into icy Alaska lake
item.description: A pilot and his two young daughters survived on the wing of a plane for about 12 hours after it crashed and was partially submerged in an icy Alaska lake, then were rescued after being spotted by a good Samaritan.
item.dc:creator: MARK THIESSEN Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Judge orders a June trial for US government's felony case against Boeing
item.description: A federal judge in Texas has set a June trial date for the U.S. government's years-old conspiracy case against Boeing for misleading regulators about the 737 Max jetliner before two of the planes crashed, killing 346 people. U.S.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Waymo plans to bring its driverless taxis to Washington in 2026
item.description: Waymo on Tuesday added Washington to its pioneering robotaxi service's steadily expanding list of U.S. markets.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Do you eat your meal in less than 20 minutes? It might be time to slow down
item.description: Experts tend to focus on the kinds of foods you can eat to improve your health. But the speed at which you devour your dinner matters just as much.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5
